terms.definition: The aggregate force—legal, social, and economic—that compels adherence to local norms
A scalar index representing how consistently the signals within a social system align with expected norms, resulting in perceived order
Any technology that lowers the epistemic cost of imagining alternative biographies by importing distant signals into the home
A regime in which a system appears orderly despite being near a critical threshold where small shocks could cause rapid change
The inverse relationship between average personal travel velocity and local social constraint pressure
The probability that observers will misattribute ongoing social order to virtue rather than residual constraints when those constraints are quietly eroding
